title: ELISA Protocol: Substrate and Stop Solution Addition
author: Opentrons

steps.title: This is the third part of a ELISA protocol
steps.instruction: This is the third part of a ELISA protocol: Substrate and Stop Solution Addition. This should be run immediately after the incubation and wash steps. Download full protocol details [here](https://s3.amazonaws.com/opentrons-protocol-library-website/custom-README-images/1464-natrix-separations-inc-milliporesigma/NEW_ELISA_protocol.xlsx). --- You will need: * P300 Multi-channel Pipette * P300 Single-channel Pipette * [Opentrons Tube Racks (2 mL Eppendorf)](https://shop.opentrons.com/collections/opentrons-tips/products/tube-rack-set-1) * [Opentrons Tube Rack (15 + 50 mL)](https://shop.opentrons.com/collections/opentrons-tips/products/tube-rack-set-1) * 96-well Plate * 12-row Trough * 300 uL Tip Racks

steps.title: Input the number of columns that...
steps.instruction: Input the number of columns that are currently occupied in the 96-well plate.

steps.title: Upload your protocol into the OT App
steps.instruction: Upload your protocol into the OT App.

steps.title: Set up your deck according to the deck map
steps.instruction: Set up your deck according to the deck map.

steps.title: Calibrate your labware, tiprack and pipette using the OT App
steps.instruction: Calibrate your labware, tiprack and pipette using the OT App. For calibration tips, check out our support article.
